ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”

translated: Then Rebekah took her best clothes, which she kept at home, and put them on Jacob her younger son. And she put the skins of the kids on his hands and on the smooth part of his neck. Then she gave the savory food and bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.

ref: Jacob knew it was Joseph's coat and said, “It's my son's coat! Joseph has been torn to pieces and eaten by some wild animal.”

translated: The woman's husband was not at home, she called out to him, “My husband, come back! They have taken Joseph and thrown him into a pit!”

ref: I will give you a lot of descendants, and they will become great nations. Some of them will even be kings.

ref: Magdiel, and Iram. These clans descended from Esau, who was known as Edom, the father of the Edomites. They took their names from the places where they settled.

translated: Magdiel and Iram. These are the leaders of Edom, according to their settlements in the land that they owned. (Esau was the father of the Edomites.)

ref: Please rescue me from my brother. I am afraid he will come and attack not only me, but my wives and children as well.

translated: The movement of the people and their noise was very much like what Esau had told him. He quickly took his bow and quiver and went to meet them to kill them if they were as fierce as Esau had described.

text: Nsu Yakob údie kenye ɔnini, “Buvele na nda-a, oowo! Si mina lɛlɔ lɛ́da-a ebiti bɔdi ni, amu ƒɔ mina etela ani na líti muute-ɔ. Ɔsika kalɛ na lɛda lɛlɛ-ɔ anumi na lɛnu-ɔ ni, use-mi aani kalɛ na lɛnu mina Unwoti Katɔti anumi. Anwuɔsu kalɛ na égyingya enu na áƒɔ-mi ku kayi ƒaa mɛni ni,
translated: The chief Yakob said to his people, “My dear friends! It is not I who have brought you this war; I have been forced into it. You know that I have always been a peaceful man and a lover of my town. But if I must choose between my town and my life, I will choose to save my town. I do not want to be the one to bring about the destruction of my town, but if it must be, then I will be the one to do it; I, Yakob, will be the one to destroy Unwoti Katɔti. If I must die, I will die after I have seen the end of this war and its effects.”

ref: Even before I had finished praying, Rebekah came by with a water jar on her shoulder. When she had filled the jar, I asked her for a drink.

translated: “She and her husband had no children. And she was very old, almost on her last breath; she had passed the age of childbearing. And she said to her husband, ‘I am now old; I will not have sexual relations with a man again.’

translated: The flood came and the flood waters rose high on the earth, and the ark floated on the flood waters. And Noah's ark rested on the mountains of Ararat. And the flood waters decreased and the land dried.

ref: May he bless you with the land he promised Abraham, so that you will take over this land where we now live as foreigners.
text: Na Unwoti Katɔti uti kusɛ kusɛ ɔni na úte Abraham ute awɔ ku ƒula kaba kemmo bebi. Na ɔya na kalɔ kani kemmo na eli ɔmwi mɛni aani ɔƒɔ kalɛ kenwini amii kadi. Kalɔ kamɛni na Katɔti úti úte Abraham.”
translated: And the righteous God said to the righteous Abraham that He would not destroy the city on account of the righteous people that were in it, and He said that He would not do it if there were fifty righteous people in it. And Abraham said to God, “If there are fifty righteous people, will you really destroy and not pardon the place for the fifty righteous people who are in it?” And God said to Abraham, “I will not destroy it if I find fifty righteous people in it.”

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:
text: Yakob ɔ́tɛ mmɔ usien ɔmmɔ. Na ala ani na ɔda ɔkɛ ni, ɔ́ƒuna etela adi ka uti ute ɔnwani Esau.
translated: And Jacob swore to him on that day, saying, 'You shall not pass on my field, and you shall not pass on my well; you shall not pass on my land; I will give you food, take this gift, and go on your way to Esau.'

ref: Rebekah still had no children. So Isaac asked the Lord to let her have a child, and the Lord answered his prayer.
text: Na Rebeka ɔmɛni ni, ɔdi udinkɔ. Anwuɔsu ni, Isak ɔ́kpali Katɔti na nwuna lili na nwuna tawɔ tawɔ ɔsu. Na Unwoti Katɔti lɛnɔ únu-nwu bɔkpali na ɔ́ya na ulekudi Rebeka úbada kaƒɔ.
translated: And Rebekah heard it, and she turned away. Isaac loved Esau because he ate of his hunting. And Rebekah loved Jacob. And Isaac said to Esau, "See, I am old; I do not know the day of my death. Now then, take your gear, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field and hunt game for me. And prepare for me the kind of food I like, and bring it to me that I may eat, that my soul may bless you before I die."
